title: Fix MongoDB Query Projection to Ignore Embedded Fields

statement.markdown: Write a MongoDB query projection function that ignores embedded fields in the `person` collection. The function should take a string `fieldPath` as input and return the projected query document. If the field path is empty, return an empty object.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing the field path, e.g. "person.name" or "".
statement.outputFormat: A MongoDB query document with the projected fields.
statement.notes: You can assume the input field path is a string in the format 'collection.field1.field2' or an empty string.

constraints.description: No constraints on the number of fields

examples.explanation: No projection when field path is empty
Project the 'name' field within the 'person' field

starterCode.python:
